context.env: Real Postgres: DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp (migrations 0001-0006 applied). turbo test task is cache:false.
invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id. Service-role Lambdas bypass RLS so they MUST scope by workspace_id in code.
workspace_id is NEVER from a client payload — it comes from the API-key/authorizer context or the trusted message body set by an earlier server step.

LOCAL-only: SES (SendEmail), SNS, and the onboarding DNS/SES-status checks are MOCKED (aws-sdk-client-mock + injected resolvers). NEVER send real mail or hit real DNS/SES. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the real local DB on localhost:5433.

Sending is gated on verification: the Dispatcher refuses to send for any workspace whose status is not active/verified (§10). Every send passes suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours, in that order.

Suppression is per-workspace: keyed (workspace_id, email); an unsubscribe/bounce in workspace A must NOT suppress the same email in workspace B. Hard bounces are ALSO recorded in the cross-workspace global_hard_bounces.
Idempotency: outbox.dedupe_key prevents double-sends; feedback handlers are idempotent on the SES message id / event.

Logic in pure injected functions; Lambda handlers stay thin and are unit-tested via the pure function. Reuse the Phase 2-5 harness (packages/db testutil: adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, applyMigrations, runPlanInWorkspaceTx) and the SqlStatement {text, values} shape.
Determinism: turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces per integration file (events.event_id is a GLOBAL PK). No cross-file races.

Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited sections require for THIS phase — no broadcasts (§9A), campaigns (§9B), image pipeline, frontend, or metering (those are later phases). Leave clean extension points.

criteria: The Dispatcher refuses to send for a workspace whose status != active/verified.
A recipient on the workspace suppression list (or in global_hard_bounces) is skipped; suppression is checked before sending.
Per-workspace frequency cap holds (a recipient over the cap within the window is not sent again); quiet-hours defer/re-queue rather than send.

A successful send is recorded once in messages_log (+ usage_counters emails_sent); the dedupe_key prevents double-sends on retry; SES is called with the workspace Configuration Set only after suppression/cap/quiet-hours pass.
scope: services/dispatcher: SQS-triggered Lambda. For each outbox row id: load profile + template (workspace-scoped); REFUSE if the workspace is not active/verified; check suppressions for (workspace_id, email) AND global_hard_bounces (skip if suppressed); enforce per-workspace frequency cap via messages_log; enforce quiet-hours (defer/re-queue); render template + inject List-Unsubscribe headers; SES SendEmail (MOCKED) with the workspace Configuration Set / sending identity; on success write messages_log (+ usage_counters emails_sent); on failure bounded retries -> DLQ.
The outbox -> second SQS queue -> Dispatcher wiring (enqueue an outbox id; Dispatcher consumes). Idempotent dedupe_key prevents double-sends.
Pure functions for the decision pipeline (suppression decision, frequency-cap, quiet-hours defer) so they are unit-tested without AWS; SES mocked with aws-sdk-client-mock.
phase.specSections: §9 (outbox -> second SQS queue -> Dispatcher: suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours -> SES send -> messages_log), §6 (outbox, messages_log, suppressions, email_templates), §10 (send gated on active/verified)
phase.title: Dispatch core

implUnits: Extend @cdp/email SesEmailClient + ProdSesEmailClient with sendEmail wrapping SESv2 SendEmailCommand (+aws-sdk-client-mock unit test) — before send path
services/dispatcher/src/core.ts pure (decideDispatch+types, isOverCap, windowStart, quiet-hours, renderTemplateBody, buildSendEmailInput, all SqlStatement builders)

services/dispatcher/src/dispatch.ts orchestrator: load outbox->workspace+profile+template scoped->canSend->isSuppressed->cap->quiet->buildSendEmailInput->ses.sendEmail->messages_log+usage+outbox-sent in ONE tx
services/dispatcher/src/deps.ts reuse runPlanInWorkspaceTx + scoped readers + ProdSesEmailClient
services/dispatcher/src/handler.ts thin SQS -> dispatch per record -> SQSBatchResponse.batchItemFailures
index.ts export handler + makeProdDeps; replace placeholder.ts
mocks: aws-sdk-client-mock over SESv2 SendEmail — assert input (ConfigurationSetName,headers)+call count, never real mail
Injected suppression/cap reader predicates + spies for order + lazy short-circuit
Synthetic SQSEvent (no real queue/LocalStack this phase)
Real Postgres localhost:5433 for integration (NOT mocked)
plan.notes: Guard ORDER is load-bearing (inv.7, §9 1-7): gate(canSend Phase6)->suppression(workspace+global_hard_bounces)->cap(messages_log window per-workspace+profile)->quiet(defer)->render+headers->SES(workspace Configuration Set)->messages_log+usage+outbox-sent ONE tx. aws-sdk-client-mock: assert SendEmail ONLY on all-pass path, NOT when refused/suppressed/capped/quiet, not twice on replay. email pkg has NO send yet — add sendEmail to SesEmailClient (own prod-impl test) first; reuse compiled_html + Phase-6 List-Unsubscribe (no hand-rolled HTML). Integration on adminPool (service-role bypass) -> in-code scoping (workspace_id $1 every stmt); reuse runPlanInWorkspaceTx for atomic messages_log+usage. Don't unit-test SQS delivery/order (§16A); synthetic events; LocalStack out of scope. Dedupe = outbox dedupe_key + atomic claim (UPDATE WHERE status=pending RETURNING). Unique workspace namespace per integration file + cleanup. Injected clock for cap/quiet determinism. global_hard_bounces has no workspace_id (cross-workspace): suppression = suppressions(workspace_id,email) OR global_hard_bounces(email).

assertions: decideDispatch send only when canSend && !suppressed && underCap && !quiet
unverified->refuse stoppedAt gate, later checks NOT consulted
suppressed->skip stoppedAt suppression
over cap->skip stoppedAt frequency_cap
quiet->defer stoppedAt quiet_hours
all pass->send
precedence gate>suppression>cap>quiet earliest stoppedAt
pure + lazy short-circuit (cap predicate not invoked if suppressed)
isOverCap count>=cap blocks, count==cap-1 sends
buildRecentSendCountQuery binds workspace_id $1+profile_id+window, messages_log status=sent
scoped by workspace_id never cross-workspace
windowStart deterministic from injected now
isInQuietHours handles midnight-wrap window
null config->never quiet
nextSendableAt returns window end (re-queue time)
uses workspace sending_identity.config_set as ConfigurationSetName
From from sending_identity never client value
body = template compiled_html + merge (no hand-rolled HTML)
List-Unsubscribe headers from Phase-6 builder, workspace-scoped URL
destination = profile email
pure
all pass -> SES SendEmail called once AFTER guards (order spy gate->suppression->cap->quiet->send)
not active/verified -> SES NOT called, no writes
suppressed -> SES NOT called, suppression before build
over cap -> NOT called
quiet -> NOT called, defer outcome
send path -> ConfigurationSetName set
dedupe guard: replay -> no re-call SES
SES failure surfaces for retry/DLQ
synthetic SQSEvent N records -> dispatch core once per record
retryable error -> batchItemFailures itemIdentifier=messageId
permanent failure bounded by outbox.attempts -> DLQ semantics
parses only outbox id; workspace_id from outbox row not SQS body
no SQS delivery/order assertions
isSuppressed true when (workspace_id,email) exists else false (adminPool)
email suppressed in A not suppressed in B
email only in global_hard_bounces suppressed for ANY workspace
query binds workspace_id $1 + email, citext case-insensitive
full dispatch core (SES mocked) to suppressed recipient writes nothing + never calls SES
seed messages_log to cap within window; count scoped workspace+profile+sent_at
at/over cap -> skip no SES no new row
rows outside window don't count -> sends
another workspace's rows for same profile don't count
success -> exactly one messages_log row status=sent + ses_message_id
usage_counters (workspace,month,emails_sent) +1 (second send ->2)
both writes in SAME tx via runPlanInWorkspaceTx (forced failure rolls back both)
all binds workspace_id $1; outbox marked sent
same outbox id twice -> one messages_log row + one usage increment
idempotency short-circuits before SES (commandCalls stays 1)
guard = outbox dedupe_key/status=sent claim-or-skip
concurrent dispatch of same outbox id -> only one sends (atomic UPDATE WHERE status=pending RETURNING)

instructions: Read CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md sections: §9 (outbox -> second SQS queue -> Dispatcher: suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours -> SES send -> messages_log), §6 (outbox, messages_log, suppressions, email_templates), §10 (send gated on active/verified).
FIRST write the failing tests from the plan (red), then implement to green. Logic lives in pure injected functions; handlers only wire them up.
Honor every invariant in context.invariants — tenant isolation, suppression scoping, the verification gate, and idempotency are pass/fail.

MOCK SES (SendEmail/identity/config-set), SNS, and DNS/SES-status checks (aws-sdk-client-mock + injected resolvers). NEVER send real mail or hit real DNS/SES. Integration tests run against the REAL local Postgres; do NOT mock the DB. Reuse the Phase 2-5 testutil harness.

Run the relevant `pnpm test` (with DATABASE_URL set), `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`. Iterate until GREEN, including a COLD turbo run. Confirm no regression in phases 2-5. Report the actual command output summary.
Implement ONLY what the cited sections require — no broadcasts/campaigns/image/frontend/metering.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { filesCreated: string[], filesModified: string[], testsPass: boolean, testSummary: string, typecheckOk: boolean, lintOk: boolean, commandsRun: string[], notes: string }
prompt.role: senior TypeScript engineer building a serverless multi-tenant CDP, test-first
prompt.task: Implement "Dispatch core" to satisfy its test plan and §18 criteria. Red -> green -> refactor.

criteria: The Dispatcher refuses to send for a workspace whose status != active/verified.
A recipient on the workspace suppression list (or in global_hard_bounces) is skipped; suppression is checked before sending.
Per-workspace frequency cap holds (a recipient over the cap within the window is not sent again); quiet-hours defer/re-queue rather than send.

A successful send is recorded once in messages_log (+ usage_counters emails_sent); the dedupe_key prevents double-sends on retry; SES is called with the workspace Configuration Set only after suppression/cap/quiet-hours pass.

For each outbox row id: load profile + template (workspace-scoped); REFUSE if the workspace is not active/verified; check suppressions for (workspace_id, email) AND global_hard_bounces (skip if suppressed); enforce per-workspace frequency cap via messages_log; enforce quiet-hours (defer/re-queue); render template + inject List-Unsubscribe headers; SES SendEmail (MOCKED) with the workspace Configuration Set / sending identity; on success write messages_log (+ usage_counters emails_sent); on failure bounded retries -> DLQ.
The outbox -> second SQS queue -> Dispatcher wiring (enqueue an outbox id; Dispatcher consumes). Idempotent dedupe_key prevents double-sends.
Pure functions for the decision pipeline (suppression decision, frequency-cap, quiet-hours defer) so they are unit-tested without AWS; SES mocked with aws-sdk-client-mock.
